# function: stddev

Returns σ = √(Σ (x − μ)² / N), the **population standard deviation**, where μ = `mean(numbers)`.

Population formula (divide by N), matching numpy's default. For sample standard deviation, take `sqrt(variance(numbers) × length(numbers) / (length(numbers) - 1))`. Errors when `numbers` is empty.

## Example Usage

```terraform
// Population standard deviation, σ = √(variance).
output "stddev" {
  value = provider::burnham::stddev([2, 4, 4, 4, 5, 5, 7, 9])
  // → 2  (population variance is 4)
}
```

## Signature

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
stddev(numbers list of number) number
```

## Arguments

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `numbers` (List of Number) A non-empty list of numbers.
